title: Definition of Impact
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Jun 21, 2010 10:19 am hey guys, i just saw this being mentioned over at the rebuild forum, and once again i seem to have managed to confuse myself. figured i might as well ask here-- but if this isn't the right topic please let me know so i can move the discussion elsewhere. ok, so according to the CI, the impacts are as follows: 1st: lilith crashes into earth. 2nd: adam goes BOOM on antarctica in 2000. 3rd: the end of the series, of course. i always found it funny that despite being canon information, there didn't seem to be a real definition of what an "impact" is supposed to be. at least, it didn't seem that way to me. going from the end of the series, i would think an "impact" would be related to instrumentality somehow. but that didn't happen in 2000-- i always assumed adam's impact was, literally, a huge explosion. this way of thinking was probably influenced by what happened to unit-04, but also i never thought an anti-ATF was involved in that incident because of three factors: a) there was no lilith component. if we go by what happened in 3I (and everything gendo and SEELE did to get to that point), however, that would mean you need adam + lilith = instrumentality. b) why would it be constrained only to antarctica? again, going by 3I an impact could've turned the whole planet into tang, but this one only affected antarctica. c) if there had been an anti-ATF involved, wouldn't Misato have gotten tanged either way? unless katsuragi sr. was crazy resourceful and actually managed to create anti-ATF resistant capsules... (would've come in handy in 2015...) also, from the resulting amount of damage (tilted earth's axis, killed half the world's population...), it's clear that there had to be a massive explosion of some sort. going by what we saw in the series, there was no explosion in 3I-- although the crazy winds revolving around the awakened unit-01 basically reduced tokyo-3 to dust, but there's no way to be sure how it affected the rest of the world. didn't seem to tilt the axis any. so, going by all this, i'm going to say the definition of "impact" does not necessarily involve SoL merging or anti-ATF. then, there was first impact: lilith hit earth. adam had apparently been around for a while-- because when lilith lost her lance, adam impaled herself on her own so lilith could populate the planet, yet 15 angels were already around but in incubation (or 14, if we go by the whole "kaworu isn't a whole new angel" idea). other than lilith's actual impact on the planet, there was no explosion, and certainly no complementation of the seeds. presumably there was no anti-ATF involved at all, because the lance put adam out of commission so why would there be any need for an anti-ATF. so, what is the definition of an "impact"? would it be defined in such general terms as something SoL-related that drastically alters the course of the planet's history? 'cause if that's the case, i really find it kind of hilarious.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Mon Jun 21, 2010 11:28 am I think an "impact" resets all life with the help of an anti at-field and creates a new ecosystem. The second impact was a failure, thus the little area concerned. And who knows, maybe Misato's capsule was thrown very far away by the explosion and the resulting anti at-field couldn't reach her? I don't think there's a tangible answer to that question.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Jun 21, 2010 1:48 pm Jinroh wrote: I think an "impact" resets all life with the help of an anti at-field and creates a new ecosystem.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Mon Jun 21, 2010 2:37 pm Huh, no? After the first impact it switched to a lilith ecosystem, except Adam frozen somewhere, its ecosystem totally disappeared.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 21, 2010 3:29 pm i always found it funny that despite being canon information, there didn't seem to be a real definition of what an "impact" is supposed to be. at least, it didn't seem that way to me.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jun 21, 2010 3:30 pm Does Adam-based life even really have an ecosystem? It's not like any of them need to eat or reproduce.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Mon Jun 21, 2010 4:41 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Adam had not yet begun to start her Biosphere when Lilith showed up. The planet was still empty.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Jun 21, 2010 5:30 pm The fact that none of the Angels had even hatched yet is a good sign.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jun 21, 2010 6:20 pm Regarding the topic of exactly what an "Impact" is, I think it's worth remembering that the term Second Impact was used as part of the coverup of Adam, claiming that it was a meteorite striking Earth that caused it. Second Impact was named such because of the cover story's similarity to First Impact. Meanwhile, Third Impact was named such because of its similarity to the real story of Second Impact. So really, "Impact" is more like a nickname when referring to 2I and 3I. The only real Impact was First Impact, because that was an object actually impacting the Earth. But I think the term "Impact" has colloquially come to refer to whatever is shared in common between 2I and 3I.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 21, 2010 6:25 pm And, you know, the exact same document that tells us about First Impact in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Mon Jun 21, 2010 6:30 pm It tells us about first impact? How and when? How is it even possible since the far didn't plan the "two seeds on the same planet" thing?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 21, 2010 8:36 pm I was referring to the Classified Information.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Tue Jun 22, 2010 4:30 am The only reference I find about the first impact in the classified informations is this: It is thought that a Spear acting as Lilith's counterpart was separated from it by the shock at the time of First Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jun 22, 2010 4:43 am Aura meant that the CI talked about 1I. Not that the DSS talked about 1I. The DSS say nothing about 1I at all. That we know of. Also, the CI does say more about 1I: "The Second Angel, known as Lilith. It came from the moon that carried the Black Moon (the former became Earth's satellite). Giant, or First, Impact was caused by the collision between the Moon and Earth. "

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Tue Jun 22, 2010 4:54 am Oh ok, so we weren't really talking about the same thing. Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Tue Jun 22, 2010 9:59 am Jinroh wrote: Huh, no? After the first impact it switched to a lilith ecosystem, except Adam frozen somewhere, its ecosystem totally disappeared.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Jun 22, 2010 11:59 am carla wrote: if an anti-ATF could be triggered using just adam, why would SEELE even bother with a scenario?

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Tue Jun 22, 2010 12:35 pm Legendary wrote: It isn't. Something called "DNA Diving" happened with Adam, which is believed to have also created Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Jun 22, 2010 12:51 pm carla wrote: ...so does that mean there was no anti-ATF involved at all?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 22, 2010 1:34 pm carla wrote: do they mention lilith specifically? gah, i need to rewatch.
